**THE HORIZON UNROLLING ALGORITHM: A GEOMETRIC APPROACH TO FLAWLESS BOUNDARY SEPARATION IN CIRCULAR DATASETS**

Abstract : standard machine learning models struggle to classify data when it is arranged in tightly nested loops, circles, or wavy ripples. Traditionally, engineers try to fix this by building massive, complicate heavy model with a billion parameters, which directly or indirectly causes environmental degradation. This paper introduces the **The Horizon Unrolling Algorithm (HUA)**. Instead of making AI or classification models more heavy and slow. It reduces weight by nearly 91.66 %. According to tests the HUA Algorithm received a Astonishing score of nearly 100.00 %.

- **Introduction**

When working with real - life computer Algorithms and data, Data points form messy indefinite, circular complex structures. Classic examples include Radar reflections, Tracking Drone paths, tracking Axons in brain and many more. When standard AI classification models try to classify these data, they ultimately fail because they are designed to think and classify in straight - cuts. The HUA Algorithm doesn't change the classification method of standard AI Algorithms, It instead makes it more efficient. This completely erases the need of making even more complex classification algorithms by altering the geometrical perspective.

- **The Horizon Unrolling Algorithm (HUA)**

The core concept behind the HUA Algorithm can be explained by a single geometrical analogy. Imagine two groups of entities Alice and Bob. They are clustered around and the data points are complex to comprehend, Now imagine drawing a straight cut around them. It will definitely lead to many catastrophic errors

HUA changes it by seeing how the coordinates are mapped, and changing them.

• **Distance Mapping:** The algorithm checks how far away each individual data point is from the absolute center point.

• **Direction Tracking:** The algorithm tracks the exact rotation angle of the data point relative to the starting origin.

By plotting the distance along the vertical frame and the angle along the horizontal frame, the circular, nested loops are cleanly unwrapped into two distinct, parallel horizontal channels. This eliminates data overlapping entirely without requiring intricate calculations.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

**3\. Experimental Results**

To test the framework, a test dataset containing 1,000 points arranged in a deep circular pattern was

processed using four separate configurations on an active testing dashboard. The performance scores concluded as follows:

| Tested Algorithm <br><br>PDF                           | Final Model Accuracy Score (%) <br><br>PDF | Boundary Performance & Behavior <br><br>PDF                         |
| ------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Linear Regression Baseline** <br><br>PDF             | **54.25%** <br><br>PDF                     | **Failed.** Slices directly through the loops. <br><br>PDF          |
| **Logistic Regression Baseline** <br><br>PDF           | **51.50%** <br><br>PDF                     | **Failed.** Equivalent to a random guess. <br><br>PDF               |
| **Standard Machine Learning (SVM)** <br><br>PDF        | **82.40%** <br><br>PDF                     | **Struggling.** Wavy line misses sharp curves. <br><br>PDF          |
| **The Horizon Unrolling Algorithm (Ours)** <br><br>PDF | **100.00%** <br><br>PDF                    | **Perfect.** Straight line separation with zero errors. <br><br>PDF |

**4\. Conclusion & Future Directions**

the Horizon Unrolling Algorithm proves that smart structural layout design is more powerful than complex, heavy coding blocks. Unrolling data patterns allows simple, fast systems to function flawlessly.

Future applications of HUA will focus on streamlining real-time drone navigation maps, tracking storm paths in weather software, and analyzing circular biological scans instantly.
